query: Recommend action movies with positive sentiment

solution_criteria: Must recommend movies with 'blockbuster' revenue tier and 'low' budget tier. Should include movies like 'Grease', 'Saw', and 'Dirty Dancing' which all achieved blockbuster status despite low budgets.

query: Compare The Mask vs Grease in terms of budget and box office performance
solution_criteria: Must compare 'The Mask' (medium budget, blockbuster revenue) vs 'Grease' (low budget, blockbuster revenue). Should highlight that both achieved blockbuster status but Grease did so with a lower budget, making it more cost-effective. May also mention production effectiveness scores (both 95) and different audience appeals (general vs family).

query: How do Saw and Scream compare on production effectiveness and audience ratings?
solution_criteria: Must compare 'Saw' vs 'Scream' on production effectiveness scores (both 90 and 75 respectively) and average ratings (3.78 vs 2.79). Should note that Saw performed better in both metrics despite both being horror movies with negative sentiment. May also compare their revenue performance (both blockbusters) and audience appeal (both teen/adult).

query: Compare the effectiveness of Dog Day Afternoon versus Heat
solution_criteria: Must compare 'Dog Day Afternoon' (production effectiveness 95, low budget, hit revenue) vs 'Heat' (production effectiveness 80, high budget, blockbuster revenue). Should note that Dog Day Afternoon achieved higher production effectiveness with much lower budget, while Heat achieved higher revenue with its larger budget investment.

query: Which performed better financially: Dirty Harry or North by Northwest?
solution_criteria: Must compare 'Dirty Harry' vs 'North by Northwest' on financial performance. Both had 'average' revenue tier and 'low' budget tier, so they performed similarly financially. Should note both achieved the same revenue tier with the same budget level, though may mention differences in ratings (4.5 vs 4.22) and production effectiveness (85 vs 85).
